detail.not-configured: No OBS address has been set, so Verger is not dialling.
detail.idle: Ready to connect. Nothing has been attempted yet.
detail.connected: Reading OBS's live state. OBS remains in charge of the stream.
detail.reconnecting: OBS went away. Verger will keep trying until it comes back.
detail.disconnected: The connection dropped and no retry is scheduled.
detail.auth-failed: OBS refused the password. Automatic retries are stopped on purpose.

connection.title: OBS connection
connection.subtitle: Verger reads OBS's state. It never imposes one — if Verger stops, OBS keeps streaming.
connection.urlHint: From OBS → Tools → WebSocket Server Settings → Show Connect Info you get a Server IP, a Port and a Password in separate boxes. Paste the IP here (or IP:port, or just the port) — it becomes ws://…:4455 for you.
connection.urlInvalid: Must be a ws:// or wss:// address.
connection.passwordHint: OBS's Server Password from the same screen. Leave blank if you turned authentication off.

notConfigured.title: OBS is not set up yet
notConfigured.body: Install OBS Studio 30 or newer, open Tools → WebSocket Server Settings, enable the server, then copy the address and password into the fields below.
notConfigured.humanTasks: The full setup checklist lives in HUMAN_TASKS.md at the root of this project.

authFailed.title: OBS rejected the password
authFailed.body: Check the password under Tools → WebSocket Server Settings in OBS, then connect again.
authFailed.noRetry: Verger has deliberately stopped retrying. Repeating a rejected password can never succeed, and a scrolling "reconnecting…" indicator would hide the real problem from you mid-service.

bridgeUnavailable.title: The privileged bridge did not load
bridgeUnavailable.body: Verger cannot reach its own main process, so OBS control is unavailable. Restart the app; if this persists, the log file will say why.

scenes.title: Scenes
scenes.readOnlyNote: Read-only. Verger reflects OBS's scene list; switching scenes arrives in a later phase.

overlay.title: Overlay
overlay.subtitle: Each layer is independent. Switching cameras never touches these, and showing one of these never touches the camera.

server.title: Overlay server
server.obsUrlLabel: Paste this into an OBS Browser Source
server.obsUrlHint: Add one Browser Source named "Overlays" to every camera scene, on top of the camera. Turn OFF "Shutdown source when not visible".

warning.noClientsBody: A layer is marked visible but no browser source is connected, so nothing is actually on screen. Check the Overlays source in OBS — it has probably been closed, hidden, or shut down. Right-click it and choose Refresh.

bridgeUnavailable.title: The privileged bridge did not load
bridgeUnavailable.body: Verger cannot reach its own main process, so the overlay server cannot be controlled. Restart the app; if this persists, the log file will say why.

lowerThird.title: Lower third
lowerThird.line2Hint: Usually the role, e.g. Worship leader. Optional.

scripture.title: Scripture
scripture.textHint: Verger never ships verse text. Paste text from your licensed provider, or let a later phase fetch it from the configured Bible API with its attribution.
scripture.translationHint: The short name shown beside the reference, e.g. 개역개정.
scripture.attributionHint: Required by most Bible licences. Leave blank if your source needs none.

slide.title: Slide
slide.srcHint: Served by the overlay server, e.g. slides/point1.png.

clear.title: Clear everything
clear.description: Blanks all three layers at once. It sits away from the SHOW buttons on purpose, and it only fires after a deliberate hold — a slip of the hand must never blank the congregation's screen.
clear.label: Clear all overlays

camera.title: Cameras
camera.subtitle: Four buttons, one live scene. Switching a camera never touches the lower third, and showing a lower third never touches the camera.
camera.noneLive: None of these four buttons is live.

unmapped.hint: Assign a scene in Camera setup before this button can be used.

camera.unmappedProgramScene: OBS is showing "{{scene}}", which none of these four buttons maps to. No button is lit on purpose — Verger reports what OBS is doing, it does not pretend.

disconnected.title: Not connected to OBS
disconnected.body: Camera switching needs a live obs-websocket connection. Open the Connection screen and connect; these buttons come back the moment OBS answers.

bridgeUnavailable.title: The privileged bridge did not load
bridgeUnavailable.body: Verger cannot reach its own main process, so cameras cannot be switched. Restart the app; if this persists, the log file will say why.

settings.title: Camera setup
settings.subtitle: Map each button to a scene OBS actually has. The scene list comes from OBS itself, so a typo can never produce a button that fires at a scene that is not there.
settings.sceneHint: Not mapped means the button stays disabled in the Cameras screen.
settings.transitionDefault: Use whatever OBS is set to
settings.transitionHint: Blank means Verger changes nothing: OBS uses its own current transition. Transitions are created and tuned in OBS; Verger only picks one by name.
settings.durationHint: Blank uses the duration configured in OBS.
settings.durationInvalid: Enter a whole number of milliseconds from 0 to 20000, or leave it blank.
settings.invalidMapping: That mapping was rejected. Check the transition duration and try again.

noScenes.title: Scenes cannot be listed until OBS connects
noScenes.body: Verger only offers scenes that OBS has reported. Connect to OBS on the Connection screen and the pickers below will fill in with your real scene names.

settings.noTransitions: OBS has not reported any transitions, so only "use whatever OBS is set to" is offered.

youtube.title: Go Live settings
youtube.subtitle: Everything the GO LIVE button will use. Nothing on this screen broadcasts anything: creating a broadcast only schedules it, and going live is a separate, later step.

notConfigured.title: YouTube is not set up yet
notConfigured.body: Verger talks to YouTube through your own Google OAuth client, never a shared one. Until GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET are present in .env there is nothing to sign in to, so every control on this screen is switched off.
notConfigured.step1: Create a project in the Google Cloud console at console.cloud.google.com.
notConfigured.step2: In that project, enable the YouTube Data API v3.
notConfigured.step3: Under APIs & Services → Credentials, create an OAuth client ID with application type "Desktop app".
notConfigured.step4: Copy the client ID and client secret into .env as GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET — the key names are already listed in .env.example — then restart Verger.
notConfigured.humanTasks: The full checklist, including the CCLI Streaming Licence number and the persistent stream key that belongs in OBS, is in HUMAN_TASKS.md at the root of this project.
notConfigured.safe: Nothing else is affected. OBS keeps streaming and recording, and every other screen works normally — only YouTube control is unavailable.

bridgeUnavailable.title: The privileged bridge did not load
bridgeUnavailable.body: Verger cannot reach its own main process, so YouTube control is unavailable. Restart the app; if this persists, the log file will say why.

account.title: Google account
account.channelUnknown: Signed in, but YouTube has not named a channel yet.
account.confirmChannel: Check this is the right channel before you go live. Google signs you in as whichever account the browser was already using, and a service published to somebody's personal channel cannot be quietly undone.
account.signInHint: Opens your browser once. Verger keeps only a refresh token, encrypted by the operating system, so later Sundays are silent.
account.signedOutHint: Verger has forgotten the stored token. Nothing can be published until you sign in again.

preflight.title: Pre-flight
preflight.allClear: No pre-flight problems. Nothing is blocking a broadcast.

youtube.not-configured: YouTube is not configured. Add GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET to .env, then restart Verger.
youtube.signed-out: Not signed in to YouTube. Sign in once on the Go Live screen; later services are silent.
youtube.authorizing: Sign-in is still in progress. Finish it in the browser window that opened.
youtube.no-persistent-stream: No persistent ingest stream is available yet, so a broadcast cannot be bound to one.
youtube.stream-unhealthy: YouTube reports the ingest as unhealthy. Check the bitrate and the network before going live.
youtube.privacy-public: This broadcast will be PUBLIC — visible to everyone and announced to subscribers. Use "unlisted" unless publishing publicly is intended; a public broadcast cannot be un-published after the fact.

legal.ccli-streaming-licence-missing: No CCLI Streaming Licence number is recorded. Streaming worship music needs a CCLI Streaming Licence — this is separate from the Church Copyright Licence. Record it in settings. (Verger is not affiliated with or endorsed by CCLI.)

preflight.blocksNote: Anything marked "blocks going live" has to be fixed before the GO LIVE button will run. Warnings are shown and left to your judgement.
preflight.ccliNote: The CCLI Streaming Licence check is a legal gate, not a reminder: streaming worship music needs a Streaming Licence in addition to the ordinary church copyright licence, and the required attribution has to go out with the stream.

template.title: Weekly template
template.titleHint: Write {date} where the date should go — it is replaced when the broadcast is created. Everything else is used exactly as typed.
template.previewEmpty: Type a title template to see how it will read.
template.descriptionHint: Shown under the video on YouTube. Service times and any required attribution belong here.

privacyOption.unlisted: Unlisted — only people with the link

template.privacyHint: Unlisted is the usual choice for a service: anyone you send the link to can watch, but it is not listed or searchable.
template.scheduledHint: Your local time. Leave it blank to schedule the broadcast for now.
template.thumbnailHint: Full path to a JPEG or PNG on this machine. Leave blank to let YouTube pick a frame.
template.timeZoneHint: IANA name, e.g. Asia/Seoul. Used to work out which day {date} means.
template.invalid: That template was rejected. A title is required and must be 100 characters or fewer.

publicWarning.title: This broadcast will be public
publicWarning.body: A public broadcast is listed, searchable, and visible to anyone the moment it starts. Publishing a service by mistake cannot be taken back — copies are already out. Choose Unlisted unless you genuinely mean to publish.

broadcast.title: This week's broadcast
broadcast.none: No broadcast has been created yet.
broadcast.createHint: Creates the scheduled broadcast on YouTube and binds the persistent stream to it. It does not start streaming — nothing goes out until GO LIVE.

stream.title: Persistent stream
stream.none: No persistent stream yet. Verger creates one the first time it makes a broadcast, then reuses it every week.
stream.explain: One ingest stream is reused forever so the settings in OBS never change and never need re-pasting. Only the broadcast is new each week.

health.noData: No data yet — OBS is not pushing

stream.keyNote: The ingest credential is deliberately not shown here and never leaves the main process — it would let anyone broadcast to this channel. It lives in OBS's own stream settings and nowhere else.

quota.title: API quota
quota.body: YouTube's Data API has a daily quota. Running a handful of services a day sits comfortably inside it, so this is not something you need to watch.

goLive.title: Go live
goLive.subtitle: One button starts the stream, the local recording and the YouTube broadcast together. One held button ends all three. The local recording always runs — there is deliberately no setting to turn it off.

bridgeUnavailable.title: The privileged bridge did not load
bridgeUnavailable.body: Verger cannot reach its own main process, so GO LIVE and END are unavailable. Anything OBS is already doing is unaffected: it keeps streaming and recording. Restart Verger, or drive OBS by hand in the meantime.

button.hint: Starts the stream and the local recording at the same moment, then makes the broadcast public once YouTube reports the ingest is healthy.

disabled.bridge: GO LIVE is switched off because Verger cannot reach its own main process.
disabled.obs: GO LIVE is switched off because Verger is not connected to OBS. Open the Connection screen and connect — nothing can be streamed or recorded until OBS answers.
disabled.starting: GO LIVE is already running. The steps below show where it has got to.
disabled.live: You are already live. Use END below to finish the service.
disabled.partial: OBS is already streaming and recording. Pressing GO LIVE again would push a second stream, so use Retry below instead.

reattached.title: Re-attached to a stream already in progress
reattached.body: OBS was already streaming or recording when Verger started, so Verger adopted that service instead of beginning a second one. Nothing was restarted, and no second recording file was opened.
reattached.elapsedNote: The elapsed time above comes from OBS, not from when you pressed GO LIVE — this copy of Verger was not running when the stream started.

partial.title: Streaming and recording — but the broadcast is NOT public on YouTube
partial.body: OBS is pushing to YouTube's ingest and writing the local recording, so nothing is being lost. What did not happen is the last step: YouTube was never switched to live, so anyone opening the link sees a waiting screen instead of the service.
partial.stillRunning: Still running: the stream to YouTube's ingest, and the local recording on this machine.
partial.notHappening: Not happening: the broadcast is not public, so nobody watching can see the service yet.
partial.retryHint: Retrying only finishes the missing steps. It never starts a second stream and never opens a second recording file.
partial.manual: If retrying keeps failing, switch the broadcast to live from YouTube Studio in a browser instead. Verger keeps streaming and recording either way — it will not interfere.

failed.title: GO LIVE did not finish
failed.body: The step marked Failed below is where it stopped. Verger has not stopped anything in response: if OBS did start streaming or recording, it is still doing both.

steps.title: Sequence

name.health: Wait for the ingest to be healthy

steps.recordNote: The local recording step has no setting and cannot be turned off. If it fails, that is a failure of GO LIVE and it is reported here rather than quietly skipped.

recordingMissing.title: The stream is running but nothing is being recorded
recordingMissing.body: OBS is pushing to YouTube and is NOT writing a local file, so this service has no backup. Verger has deliberately not stopped the stream over this. Press Start Recording in OBS's main window, and check the recording folder under Settings → Output.

recordingFile.title: Recording file
recordingFile.none: OBS has not reported a path yet. It normally appears a second or two after the recording starts.
recordingFile.note: This file is your backup. It keeps being written even if the internet drops, if YouTube refuses the broadcast, or if Verger itself crashes — OBS owns it, not Verger.

health.title: Health
health.reconnectingBody: The RTMP connection dropped and OBS is retrying it by itself. The local recording is unaffected and keeps running throughout.
health.bitrateNote: A dropped-frame count that keeps climbing means the upload cannot keep up. Lower the video bitrate in OBS rather than letting frames pile up — the bitrate tiers are in docs/v2-notes/NETWORK_AND_HARDWARE.md.

end.title: End the service
end.description: Marks the YouTube broadcast finished, stops the stream, and stops the local recording. It sits well away from GO LIVE on purpose and only fires after a deliberate hold: ending a service by a mis-click cannot be undone, and the congregation watching at home saw it end.
end.label: END the service
end.nothingToEnd: Nothing is streaming or recording, so there is nothing to end.

youtubeNotConfigured.title: YouTube is not set up — GO LIVE will stream and record, but publish nothing
youtubeNotConfigured.body: There is no GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID or GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET in .env, so Verger has no YouTube account to talk to. GO LIVE still works, and it is worth being exact about what it will and will not do.
youtubeNotConfigured.willLabel: What GO LIVE will still do
youtubeNotConfigured.will1: Tell OBS to start streaming, to whatever destination OBS's own Stream settings point at.
youtubeNotConfigured.will2: Tell OBS to start the local recording, exactly as it always does — that never depends on YouTube.
youtubeNotConfigured.willNotLabel: What GO LIVE will not do
youtubeNotConfigured.willNot1: Create a broadcast on YouTube, or bind an ingest stream to one.
youtubeNotConfigured.willNot2: Make anything public. No watch link is produced and no viewer is notified.
youtubeNotConfigured.where: Set it up on the Go Live settings tab. The full checklist, including the CCLI Streaming Licence number, is in HUMAN_TASKS.md at the root of this project.

quota.title: API quota
quota.body: YouTube's Data API has a daily quota. Going live and ending again costs a handful of units, so a few services a day sits comfortably inside it — this is not something you need to watch.

plan.title: Service plan
plan.subtitle: The order of service, as a list of cues. Every cue here is fired by hand — this screen works with no microphone, no cue engine and no network, and it is what everything later falls back to.
plan.notStarted: Not started. Press SPACE to fire the first cue.
plan.endOfPlan: End of the plan. Nothing follows.

empty.title: No cues in this service plan yet.
empty.body: Author the order of service in the plan editor — or import a PowerPoint deck — then come back here to run it.
empty.noBridge: Verger cannot reach its main process, so no plan can be loaded. Restart the app.

preview.malformed: This cue’s settings do not match its type. Fix it in the plan editor.
preview.scriptureResolved: Verse text is fetched when the cue fires.

file.label: Plan file
file.neverSaved: Not saved to a file yet

list.label: Cues, in order
list.heading: Cues ({{total}})
list.reorderHint: Reorder with the grip handle: drag it, or focus it and press Space, then the arrow keys, then Space again.
list.empty: No cues yet. Add one, or import a deck.

trigger.label: Trigger
trigger.modeHint: Manual is the default and the only mode this build acts on. The others are armed for a later phase and change nothing yet.
trigger.textRequired: A {{mode}} trigger needs text to match against, or it will never fire.
trigger.manualNote: You fire this cue yourself, with its play button or by advancing through the plan.

payload.sceneHint: Must match a scene that exists in OBS, exactly.
payload.slideAssetHint: Path inside the plan's asset folder, e.g. slides/slide-001.png.
payload.mediaAssetHint: Path inside the plan's asset folder, or leave it and drive an OBS media input instead.
payload.sourceSlide: Slide number in the imported deck
payload.actionHint: A named app action, for example overlay.clear.

plan.scriptureNote: There is no box for the verse itself, on purpose. Verger stores only the reference; the wording is fetched from a licensed source at the moment the cue fires and is never written into the plan file.

options.title: Automation
options.autoFireThresholdHint: How sure a match must be before this cue may fire itself in auto mode. It has no effect on a manual trigger.
options.confirmAlwaysHint: Beats the service-wide mode. A cue can always be made safer than the default, never more dangerous — use it for anything you would hate to see appear by itself.

editor.title: Cue
editor.none: Select a cue to edit it.
editor.labelHint: What you will read in the list mid-service. Keep it short.
editor.noteHint: For you only. Never shown on the congregation screen.
editor.invalid: This cue is not valid yet and will not save. Check the fields marked below.

unavailable.title: No PowerPoint converter is installed, so deck import is switched off
unavailable.noDetail: Verger found no converter on this machine.
unavailable.install: Install LibreOffice (it is free) and restart Verger. Verger uses it only to turn each slide into an image; it never reads the words on your slides.
unavailable.meanwhile: Until then you can still build the service by hand: export the deck to PNG or JPEG yourself, drop the images into the plan's asset folder, and add one slide cue per image.

progress.label: Import progress
progress.writing: Writing images into the asset folder

bridgeUnavailable.body: Verger's privileged bridge did not load, so the plan cannot be saved, opened or fired. The cues shown are read-only. Restarting the app usually fixes this.

error.title: The last plan action was refused

panel.title: Transcript
panel.subtitle: What speech recognition is hearing from the pulpit microphone. Verger never needs it to run the service.
panel.emptyHint: Press Start listening once the pulpit microphone is live. Nothing else waits on this — cues, cameras and overlays all work without it.
panel.draftHint: Draft and settling lines are still being revised and will be replaced in place. Only plain lines are final.

degraded.title: Running on {{provider}}
degraded.body: The preferred provider stopped responding, so recognition fell back. A transcript is still arriving — expect it to be slower and a little less accurate.

failed.title: Speech recognition failed
failed.body: No transcript is arriving. Verger is running manual and nothing is blocked: every cue, camera and overlay still works exactly as it does without speech.

notConfigured.title: Speech recognition is not set up
notConfigured.body: No speech provider is configured, so there is no transcript. Verger is running manual and nothing is blocked. Set a provider up under Speech settings when you want one.

bridgeUnavailable.title: Speech recognition is unavailable
bridgeUnavailable.body: Verger's privileged bridge did not load, so the microphone cannot be reached. Everything else on this screen keeps working.

panel.micUnavailable: The microphone could not be opened: {{reason}}

settings.title: Speech settings
settings.subtitle: Which engine transcribes the service, which microphone it listens to, and which words it should expect.
settings.modeHint: Cloud and local fail in opposite ways, which is why both exist.

modeDetail.cloud: Deepgram. Lower latency and noticeably better at Korean — but it dies with the internet, and the internet is already carrying the stream.
modeDetail.local: faster-whisper on this machine. Keeps working with no network at all; needs a capable GPU and is slower.
modeDetail.auto: Cloud while it is healthy, falling back to local the moment it is not. Recommended.

settings.languageHint: The language spoken from the pulpit. This is separate from the language of Verger's own buttons.
settings.deviceHint: Pick the pulpit microphone, not the webcam. Device names appear once you have allowed microphone access at least once.
settings.deviceUnavailable: This machine did not report any audio inputs. The system default input will be used.
settings.modelHint: Larger is more accurate and slower, and must fit in the graphics card's memory. This machine has 4 GB, so 'small' is the sensible ceiling and 'large-v3' will not load.

vocabulary.hint: The single most effective setting here. Add the pastor's name, the church name, hymn titles and any term that comes up week after week — boosting them sharply improves accuracy on exactly the words that matter.
vocabulary.examples: These are the words a general-purpose model has never seen, so they are the ones it gets wrong.

rejected.duplicate: That term is already in the list.
rejected.too-long: That term is too long. Keep it under 80 characters.

settings.invalid: These settings could not be saved as written. Check the vocabulary list and the model name.

notConfigured.title: No speech provider is configured
notConfigured.body: DEEPGRAM_API_KEY is empty, so cloud recognition is switched off. This is not a fault — Verger simply runs manual and the rest of the console is unaffected.
notConfigured.humanTasks: Adding the key is a human-only task: see HUMAN_TASKS.md in the project folder. Local recognition can be used instead by choosing Local or Auto above.

modeDetail.assist: Verger highlights the next likely cue and waits for you to confirm it with a tap, a key or a pedal. Nothing reaches the congregation screen until you say so.
modeDetail.auto: High-confidence cues fire on their own, without asking. Lower-confidence ones still wait for you.
modeDetail.manual: You drive. Detections are still shown so the transcript stays useful, but nothing is ever offered as an action to accept.

alignmentDetail.aligned: The recent transcript matches the cues just ahead, so the plan-follower is tracking.
alignmentDetail.lost: The speaker has left the script, so the plan-follower is quietly waiting. Scripture and hot-phrase detection keep working — they need no plan at all.
alignmentDetail.no-plan: No plan with anchor text is loaded, so there is nothing to follow. Scripture and hot-phrase detection still work.

suggestion.cueMissing: The cue this points at is not in the plan any more, so there is nothing to fire. Dismiss it.
suggestion.attributionNone: This source asks for no attribution.
suggestion.textUnavailableDetail: The verse text could not be fetched, so this will NOT be shown automatically in any mode. Showing it now would put an empty scripture card in front of the congregation.
suggestion.textNote: Verger never stores verse text. What is fetched is shown once, with its attribution, and never written into your plan file.
suggestion.willAutoFire: This will fire by itself in a moment. Press N, or Dismiss, to stop it.
suggestion.announce: Suggestion: {{what}}. {{why}}. Press Y to confirm, N to dismiss.
suggestion.noneDetail: The engine is watching. A card appears here the moment it has something to offer.
suggestion.undoHint: Steps the plan back one cue. It never blanks the congregation screen.
suggestion.error: The last automation action was refused

trust.title: Trust dial
trust.subtitle: How much Verger may do without asking. Assist is the default, and it is the one to run.
trust.autoCaution: Caution: in Auto, a high-confidence cue appears on the congregation screen without asking you first. You can veto it, but only once it is already up.
trust.perCueNote: A single cue can always be made safer than this setting — tick “Always ask me first” on it in the plan — but never more dangerous.

panic.regionLabel: PANIC — halts automation only; it does not stop the stream, does not stop the recording, and does not clear the screen
panic.title: PANIC
panic.label: PANIC — stop automation, not the stream
panic.does: Halts every automatic action at once: no cue fires, no scripture appears, and no suggestion is applied.
panic.notStream: It does not stop the stream. You stay live.
panic.notRecording: It does not stop the local recording. Your backup keeps being written.
panic.notScreen: It does not clear the congregation screen. Whatever is showing stays showing.
panic.reassurance: That is why it is safe to press. It costs you nothing except the automation you did not want.
panic.panickedBody: Verger is firing nothing and offering nothing. The stream, the local recording and the congregation screen are exactly as you left them.
panic.resumeHint: Nothing turns automation back on by itself — not a new suggestion, not a mode change, not restarting speech recognition. Only this button.
panic.lockedWhilePanicked: The trust dial is locked while automation is halted. Resume first.

hotPhrases.title: Hot phrases
hotPhrases.subtitle: A phrase spoken from the pulpit, bound to a cue. It needs no plan and no alignment, so it keeps working in fully extemporaneous preaching.
hotPhrases.emptyHint: Add the things said the same way every week — “let’s pray”, “받으실 말씀은”.
hotPhrases.noCues: There are no cues to bind to yet. Author the service plan first: a hot phrase with nothing to fire would do nothing.
hotPhrases.tooShort: That phrase is too short to save. Use at least {{min}} characters.
hotPhrases.shortWarning: Very short — this will fire when nobody meant it to. A phrase under {{min}} characters turns up inside ordinary words, so make it a whole spoken phrase.
hotPhrases.duplicate: That phrase is already in the list.
hotPhrases.saveFailed: That change was refused and has not been saved.

bridgeUnavailable.title: The cue engine is unavailable
bridgeUnavailable.body: Verger cannot reach its own main process, so nothing is being detected and nothing can fire. The plan, the cameras and the overlay all still work by hand.

boundary.title: This screen stopped working
boundary.body: Verger's control surface hit an unexpected error. OBS is unaffected — the stream and the local recording are still running.
boundary.reloadHint: Reloading restores the control surface and reconnects to whatever state OBS is in.

health.title: Subsystem status
health.serviceQuestion: Is the service still going out?
health.serviceYes: Yes — the service is still going out.
health.serviceNo: No — nothing is going out right now.
health.serviceYesDetail: The stream, the local recording, or both are still running. Whatever else on this page is red, the congregation is still being served — do not stop anything to investigate.
health.serviceNoDetail: Neither the stream nor the local recording is running. This is the one state worth interrupting for.
health.attentionNone: Nothing needs attention. Anything listed as not set up is resting, not broken.
health.bridgeUnavailable: Verger cannot reach its own main process, so these lights are not being updated. Restart the app. OBS is unaffected and keeps streaming and recording.

recovery.label: Recovery
recovery.noBroadcastImpact: Nothing on this panel can stop the stream or the recording. Both keep running through every action here.
recovery.reloadOverlaysDetail: Forces every attached OBS browser source to reload and re-sync from Verger. Use this when an overlay is stuck or blank. The camera feed and the broadcast are untouched.
recovery.reloadFailed: The overlays could not be reloaded. Nothing else changed.
recovery.restoreFailed: That checkpoint could not be restored. Automation was left exactly as it was.

checkpoint.detail: Pick the last point where automation was doing the right thing. Newest first.
checkpoint.none: No checkpoints have been recorded yet.
checkpoint.nonEffects: This rewinds automation only — the plan pointer and what Verger will do next. It does NOT touch the stream and it does NOT touch the recording. Nothing the congregation is watching changes.

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts, foot pedals & Stream Deck
shortcuts.subtitle: Every operator action can be triggered by a key. Changes apply as soon as you save.
shortcuts.tableCaption: Actions and the keys bound to them
shortcuts.repaired: Some saved shortcuts could not be read and were put back to their defaults.
shortcuts.conflict: Conflict: {{chord}} is bound to {{actions}}. Only the first one will fire.

pedal.title: Foot pedals and Stream Decks
pedal.body: A foot pedal or Stream Deck is just a keyboard: it sends an ordinary key code. So there is nothing extra to install — choose an action below, press Rebind, and then press the pedal or the Stream Deck button itself. Verger records whatever key it sends. If your pedal has configuration software, set it to send a key nothing else uses, such as F13.

capture.prompt: Press the key, foot pedal or Stream Deck button you want. Press Tab to cancel.

reject.destructiveTap: {{action}} can blank the output, so it must be a hold, never a tap.
reject.holdTooShort: {{action}} can blank the output, so it must be held for at least {{ms}} ms.
reject.badHold: Enter a hold between {{floor}} and {{max}} milliseconds.

card.title: Shortcut card
card.body: Print this and tape it to the booth keyboard. The blueprint asks for it by name: the key that hands control back to the human must be readable without opening the app.

tuning.title: Confidence tuning
tuning.subtitle: These numbers decide how sure Verger has to be before it does anything on its own. Every one of them is a trade: raise it and the console asks you more often, lower it and the system will act on less certainty.
tuning.fixedHint: Fixed at {{value}} in this build — changing it is a code change, not a setting.

scope.title: What this screen changes
scope.body: Only the default auto-fire threshold is saved in this build. The scripture bands, the plan anchor threshold and the auto-fire gap are compiled into Verger and are shown here read-only, at their real values, so you can see what the system is actually working to.

autoFire.label: Default auto-fire threshold
autoFire.plain: In auto mode, a cue may fire itself only when the match is at least this confident. Cues below it are offered to you instead.
autoFire.consequence: Lower this and the system will act on less certainty: more cues fire without you, and more of the ones that fire will be wrong — on the congregation screen, where you cannot take them back.
autoFire.looseWarning: This is looser than recommended. Verger will act on less certainty than the setting this build was tested with.

scripture.exactPlain: At or above this, a spoken reference is treated as exact. Only an exact reference is ever allowed to show itself, and only once its text has actually been fetched.
scripture.exactConsequence: Lower this and the system will act on less certainty: a near-miss gets treated as a certainty, so the wrong passage can go up while the right one is being read.
scripture.fuzzyPlain: Between this and the exact band, a reference is offered to you flagged as uncertain. Below it, the guess is discarded rather than shown.
scripture.fuzzyConsequence: Lower this and the system will act on less certainty: the panel fills with weak guesses, and a panel full of noise is a panel you stop reading — which is worse than missing one reference.

plan.anchorPlain: How closely what was said must match a cue’s anchor phrase before the plan pointer moves to it. Deliberately stricter than the scripture bands.
plan.anchorConsequence: Lower this and the system will act on less certainty: the follower jumps on a loose resemblance, which is how a service ends up three slides ahead of the preacher.
plan.gapPlain: The floor between two automatic fires, in milliseconds. It is also the window you have to veto one.
plan.gapConsequence: Lower this and the system will act faster than you can stop it: a burst of transcript can fire three cues in a second and the congregation watches the slides flicker past.

overrides.title: Per-cue overrides
overrides.hint: Cues in the open plan that set their own rules. These are authored in the plan editor and saved in the plan file — this list is here so you can see them all in one place.
overrides.empty: No cue in this plan overrides the service default.
overrides.confirmAlways: Always asks first. This cue can never fire on its own, whatever the mode.
overrides.looseWarning: Looser than the service default — this cue will act on less certainty than the rest of the service.
